key_insight: Midpoint-fused arches can exhibit bistability, allowing for two distinct, stable equilibrium states.
engineering_relevance: Understanding the bistability of MFA can aid in designing innovative structural systems with tunable mechanical properties.
method: Developed an analytical model minimizing total potential energy.
Derived kinematic compatibility relations at the fusion point for coupled deformations.
Conducted nonlinear finite-element simulations and table-top experiments.
evidence_signal: Analytical predictions are corroborated by simulations and experiments, showing close agreement in equilibrium states and transition responses.
watch_item: The complexity of deformation pathways may pose challenges for practical implementation in real-world applications.
paper_title: Bistability of midpoint-fused arches with pinned-pinned boundary conditions
